papers.title: Extending One-Step Image Generation from Class Labels to Text via Discriminative Text Representation
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers extend MeanFlow generation from class labels to text inputs by integrating powerful LLM-based text encoders, overcoming limitations of few-step refinement through enhanced semantic feature representation.  AI-generated summary Few-step generation has been a long-standing goal, with recent one-step generation methods exemplified by MeanFlow achieving remarkable results. Existing research on MeanFlow primarily focuses on class-to-image generation. However, an intuitive yet unexplored direction is to extend the condition from fixed class labels to flexible text inputs, enabling richer content creation. Compared to the limited class labels, text conditions pose greater challenges to the model's understanding capability, necessitating the effective integration of powerful text encoders into the MeanFlow framework. Surprisingly, although incorporating text conditions appears straightforward, we find that integrating powerful LLM-based text encoders using conventional training strategies results in unsatisfactory performance. To uncover the underlying cause, we conduct detailed analyses and reveal that, due to the extremely limited number of refinement steps in the MeanFlow generation, such as only one step, the text feature representations are required to possess sufficiently high discriminability. This also explains why discrete and easily distinguishable class features perform well within the MeanFlow framework. Guided by these insights, we leverage a powerful LLM-based text encoder validated to possess the required semantic properties and adapt the MeanFlow generation process to this framework, resulting in efficient text-conditioned synthesis for the first time. Furthermore, we validate our approach on the widely used diffusion model, demonstrating significant generation performance improvements. We hope this work provides a general and practical reference for future research on text-conditioned MeanFlow generation. The code is available at https://github.com/AMAP-ML/EMF.
affiliations: College of Computer Science, Nankai University

ru.title: От класса к тексту: дискриминативные признаки для однош агового синтеза
ru.desc: Исследователи расширили метод MeanFlow для генерации изображений с текстовыми условиями вместо классовых меток, интегрировав мощные текстовые энкодеры на основе LLM. Авторы обнаружили, что для однош агового или мало-шагового поколения требуется высокая дискриминативность признаков текста, что объясняет трудности при наивном внедрении. Они предложили специализированный подход к адаптации процесса MeanFlow для работы с семантически богатыми представлениями LLM-энкодеров. Метод впервые обеспечивает эффективный синтез изображений с текстовыми условиями в рамках MeanFlow, достигая значительных улучшений производительности и демонстрируя универсальность на диффузионных моделях.

en.title: Transforming MeanFlow: From Class Labels to Text Inputs
en.desc: This paper presents an advancement in the MeanFlow generation technique by extending its application from class labels to text inputs using large language model (LLM) based text encoders. The authors identify that traditional training methods for integrating text encoders lead to poor performance due to the limited refinement steps in the MeanFlow process. They conduct analyses to show that high discriminability in text feature representations is crucial for effective generation. By adapting the MeanFlow framework with a powerful LLM-based text encoder, the researchers achieve significant improvements in text-conditioned synthesis, paving the way for richer content generation.

zh.title: 文本条件生成的新突破
zh.desc: 本研究将MeanFlow生成方法从类标签扩展到文本输入，利用强大的基于大语言模型（LLM）的文本编码器，克服了少步精炼的局限性。研究表明，文本条件的引入使得内容生成更加丰富，但也对模型的理解能力提出了更高的挑战。通过详细分析，我们发现传统训练策略下，直接整合文本编码器会导致性能不佳。最终，我们成功地将MeanFlow生成过程适配到新的框架中，实现了高效的文本条件合成，并在广泛使用的扩散模型上验证了显著的生成性能提升。

papers.title: OneVL: One-Step Latent Reasoning and Planning with Vision-Language Explanation
papers.abstract: Abstract OneVL presents a unified vision-language-action framework that improves latent chain-of-thought reasoning for autonomous driving by integrating language and visual world model supervision for faster, more accurate trajectory prediction.  AI-generated summary Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning has become a powerful driver of trajectory prediction in VLA-based autonomous driving, yet its autoregressive nature imposes a latency cost that is prohibitive for real-time deployment. Latent CoT methods attempt to close this gap by compressing reasoning into continuous hidden states, but consistently fall short of their explicit counterparts. We suggest that this is due to purely linguistic latent representations compressing a symbolic abstraction of the world, rather than the causal dynamics that actually govern driving. Thus, we present OneVL (One-step latent reasoning and planning with Vision-Language explanations), a unified VLA and World Model framework that routes reasoning through compact latent tokens supervised by dual auxiliary decoders. Alongside a language decoder that reconstructs text CoT, we introduce a visual world model decoder that predicts future-frame tokens, forcing the latent space to internalize the causal dynamics of road geometry, agent motion, and environmental change. A three-stage training pipeline progressively aligns these latents with trajectory, language, and visual objectives, ensuring stable joint optimization. At inference, the auxiliary decoders are discarded and all latent tokens are prefilled in a single parallel pass, matching the speed of answer-only prediction. Across four benchmarks, OneVL becomes the first latent CoT method to surpass explicit CoT, delivering state-of-the-art accuracy at answer-only latency, and providing direct evidence that tighter compression, when guided in both language and world-model supervision, produces more generalizable representations than verbose token-by-token reasoning. Project Page: https://xiaomi-embodied-intelligence.github.io/OneVL

ru.title: Скрытое рассуждение с двойной супервизией для быстрого предсказания траекторий
ru.desc: OneVL представляет объединённую систему для автономного вождения, которая улучшает скрытое цепное рассуждение путём интеграции языковой и визуальной супервизии мировой модели. Метод компрессирует процесс рассуждения в компактные скрытые состояния, заставляя модель изучать причинную динамику дорожной геометрии и движения агентов через два вспомогательных декодера. Трёхэтапный конвейер обучения прогрессивно согласует скрытые представления с целями предсказания траектории, языка и визуальной информации. В результате OneVL впервые демонстрирует, что скрытое цепное рассуждение может превзойти явное рассуждение, достигая наилучшей точности при скорости инференса ответов без рассуждений.

en.title: OneVL: Fast and Accurate Trajectory Prediction for Autonomous Driving
en.desc: OneVL is a new framework that combines vision, language, and action to enhance reasoning for autonomous driving. It addresses the latency issues of traditional Chain-of-Thought (CoT) methods by using a latent representation that captures the causal dynamics of driving rather than just symbolic abstractions. The framework employs dual auxiliary decoders for language and visual predictions, allowing for efficient trajectory forecasting. By training on multiple objectives, OneVL achieves superior accuracy and speed, outperforming previous methods in real-time applications.

zh.title: 统一视觉-语言-行动框架提升自动驾驶推理速度与准确性
zh.desc: OneVL是一个统一的视觉-语言-行动框架，旨在通过整合语言和视觉世界模型的监督，提升自动驾驶中的潜在链式思维推理。该方法通过压缩推理为连续的隐藏状态，解决了传统链式思维推理在实时部署中的延迟问题。OneVL引入了双辅助解码器，结合语言解码器和视觉世界模型解码器，使潜在空间能够内化道路几何、代理运动和环境变化的因果动态。经过三阶段的训练，OneVL在多个基准测试中超越了显式链式思维推理，展现出更高的准确性和更快的推理速度。

papers.title: Agent-World: Scaling Real-World Environment Synthesis for Evolving General Agent Intelligence
papers.abstract: Abstract Agent-World introduces a self-evolving training framework that advances general agent intelligence through autonomous environment discovery and continuous learning across diverse real-world scenarios.  AI-generated summary Large language models are increasingly expected to serve as general-purpose agents that interact with external, stateful tool environments. The Model Context Protocol (MCP) and broader agent skills offer a unified interface for connecting agents with scalable real-world services, but training robust agents remains limited by the lack of realistic environments and principled mechanisms for life-long learning. In this paper, we present Agent-World, a self-evolving training arena for advancing general agent intelligence through scalable environments. Agent-World has two main components: (1) Agentic Environment-Task Discovery, which autonomously explores topic-aligned databases and executable tool ecosystems from thousands of real-world environment themes and synthesizes verifiable tasks with controllable difficulty; and (2) Continuous Self-Evolving Agent Training, which combines multi-environment reinforcement learning with a self-evolving agent arena that automatically identifies capability gaps through dynamic task synthesis and drives targeted learning, enabling the co-evolution of agent policies and environments. Across 23 challenging agent benchmarks, Agent-World-8B and 14B consistently outperforms strong proprietary models and environment scaling baselines. Further analyses reveal scaling trends in relation to environment diversity and self-evolution rounds, offering insights for building general agent intelligence.

ru.title: Самоэволюционирующая тренировочная арена для универсальных агентов
ru.desc: Agent-World представляет самоэволюционирующую платформу для обучения универсальных агентов, которая автоматически открывает и синтезирует разнообразные окружения из реальных источников. Система использует два ключевых компонента: автономное обнаружение задач из тематически организованных баз данных и экосистем инструментов, а также непрерывное обучение с подкреплением в многосредовой среде. Платформа применяет динамическое создание задач для выявления пробелов в способностях агента и целенаправленного улучшения его политик. Эксперименты показывают, что модели Agent-World-8B и 14B превосходят сильные проприетарные решения и демонстрируют положительные тренды масштабирования по разнообразию окружений.

en.title: Empowering Agents Through Self-Evolving Training in Diverse Environments
en.desc: The paper presents Agent-World, a novel framework designed to enhance general agent intelligence through self-evolving training. It features two key components: the Agentic Environment-Task Discovery, which autonomously identifies and creates tasks from diverse real-world themes, and Continuous Self-Evolving Agent Training, which utilizes multi-environment reinforcement learning to adaptively improve agent capabilities. This approach allows agents to continuously learn and evolve by identifying their weaknesses and adjusting their training accordingly. The results show that Agent-World significantly outperforms existing models across various benchmarks, highlighting its effectiveness in fostering robust agent development.

zh.title: 自我进化的智能体训练框架
zh.desc: Agent-World 是一个自我进化的训练框架，旨在通过自主环境发现和持续学习来提升通用智能体的能力。它包含两个主要部分：一是自主探索与主题相关的数据库和可执行工具生态系统，生成可验证的任务；二是结合多环境强化学习的自我进化智能体训练，自动识别能力差距并推动有针对性的学习。通过动态任务合成，Agent-World 使智能体策略和环境能够共同进化。实验结果显示，Agent-World 在 23 个具有挑战性的基准测试中表现优于其他强大的专有模型。

papers.title: OpenGame: Open Agentic Coding for Games
papers.abstract: Abstract OpenGame is an open-source agentic framework for end-to-end web game creation that uses specialized code models and evaluation benchmarks to overcome challenges in interactive application development.  AI-generated summary Game development sits at the intersection of creative design and intricate software engineering, demanding the joint orchestration of game engines, real-time loops, and tightly coupled state across many files. While Large Language Models (LLMs) and code agents now solve isolated programming tasks with ease, they consistently stumble when asked to produce a fully playable game from a high-level design, collapsing under cross-file inconsistencies, broken scene wiring, and logical incoherence. We bridge this gap with OpenGame, the first open-source agentic framework explicitly designed for end-to-end web game creation. At its core lies Game Skill, a reusable, evolving capability composed of a Template Skill that grows a library of project skeletons from experience and a Debug Skill that maintains a living protocol of verified fixes - together enabling the agent to scaffold stable architectures and systematically repair integration errors rather than patch isolated syntax bugs. Powering this framework is GameCoder-27B, a code LLM specialized for game engine mastery through a three-stage pipeline of continual pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, and execution-grounded reinforcement learning. Since verifying interactive playability is fundamentally harder than checking static code, we further introduce OpenGame-Bench, an evaluation pipeline that scores agentic game generation along Build Health, Visual Usability, and Intent Alignment via headless browser execution and VLM judging. Across 150 diverse game prompts, OpenGame establishes a new state-of-the-art. We hope OpenGame pushes code agents beyond discrete software engineering problems and toward building complex, interactive real-world applications. Our framework will be fully open-sourced.

ru.title: Агентные системы для автоматического создания интерактивных приложений
ru.desc: OpenGame — это открытая фреймворк для автоматической генерации веб-игр, решающая проблему создания полнофункциональных интерактивных приложений с помощью специализированных языковых моделей. В основе фреймворка лежит Game Skill — переиспользуемая возможность, объединяющая Template Skill (библиотека архитектурных шаблонов) и Debug Skill (протокол верифицированных исправлений), которые позволяют агенту строить стабильные системы и систематически исправлять ошибки интеграции. Ядром системы является GameCoder-27B — специализированная языковая модель для кодирования игр, обученная через трёхэтапный pipeline, включающий continual pre-training, supervised fine-tuning и reinforcement learning на основе выполнения кода. Для оценки качества генерируемых игр представлена OpenGame-Bench — benchmark, проверяющий стабильность сборки, визуальную пригодность и соответствие исходному замыслу через headless браузер и визуальные языковые модели.

en.title: Revolutionizing Game Development with AI
en.desc: OpenGame is an innovative framework designed for creating web games using advanced machine learning techniques. It addresses the challenges faced by AI in generating fully playable games from high-level designs, which often result in inconsistencies and logical errors. The framework utilizes Game Skill, which includes a Template Skill for generating project structures and a Debug Skill for fixing integration issues, allowing for more stable game development. Powered by the GameCoder-27B model and supported by the OpenGame-Bench evaluation system, OpenGame sets a new standard in automated game creation by ensuring both functionality and user experience.

zh.title: OpenGame：开创网页游戏开发的新纪元
zh.desc: OpenGame是一个开源的智能框架，旨在实现端到端的网页游戏创建。它利用专门的代码模型和评估基准，解决互动应用开发中的挑战。通过Game Skill，OpenGame能够构建稳定的游戏架构并系统性地修复集成错误，而不仅仅是修补语法错误。该框架由GameCoder-27B驱动，专注于游戏引擎的掌握，并通过OpenGame-Bench评估生成的游戏质量。

papers.title: MultiWorld: Scalable Multi-Agent Multi-View Video World Models
papers.abstract: Abstract MultiWorld is a unified framework for multi-agent multi-view world modeling that achieves accurate multi-agent control while maintaining multi-view consistency through specialized modules for condition handling and global state encoding.  AI-generated summary Video world models have achieved remarkable success in simulating environmental dynamics in response to actions by users or agents. They are modeled as action-conditioned video generation models that take historical frames and current actions as input to predict future frames. Yet, most existing approaches are limited to single-agent scenarios and fail to capture the complex interactions inherent in real-world multi-agent systems. We present MultiWorld, a unified framework for multi-agent multi-view world modeling that enables accurate control of multiple agents while maintaining multi-view consistency. We introduce the Multi-Agent Condition Module to achieve precise multi-agent controllability, and the Global State Encoder to ensure coherent observations across different views. MultiWorld supports flexible scaling of agent and view counts, and synthesizes different views in parallel for high efficiency. Experiments on multi-player game environments and multi-robot manipulation tasks demonstrate that MultiWorld outperforms baselines in video fidelity, action-following ability, and multi-view consistency. Project page: https://multi-world.github.io/

ru.title: Согласованное моделирование мира для многоагентного управления с разными точками зрения
ru.desc: MultiWorld представляет собой унифицированную архитектуру для моделирования динамики окружения с несколькими агентами и множественными ракурсами. Система использует специализированный модуль управления условиями для точного контроля каждого агента и глобальный кодировщик состояния для обеспечения согласованности наблюдений с разных ракурсов. Фреймворк позволяет гибко масштабировать количество агентов и представлений, одновременно синтезируя различные виды сцены. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходство MultiWorld в качестве видео, следовании действиям и согласованности между ракурсами на задачах игровых окружений и многороботной манипуляции.

en.title: MultiWorld: Mastering Multi-Agent Control with Consistent Views
en.desc: MultiWorld is a framework designed for modeling environments where multiple agents interact from different perspectives. It enhances multi-agent control by using a Multi-Agent Condition Module that allows precise control over each agent's actions. Additionally, the Global State Encoder ensures that observations from various views remain consistent, which is crucial for accurate decision-making. Experiments show that MultiWorld excels in generating high-quality video outputs and maintaining coherence across multiple views, outperforming existing methods in complex scenarios.

zh.title: MultiWorld：多智能体的统一建模框架
zh.desc: MultiWorld是一个统一的框架，用于多智能体多视角的世界建模。它通过专门的模块处理条件和全局状态编码，实现了准确的多智能体控制，同时保持多视角的一致性。该框架引入了多智能体条件模块，以实现精确的多智能体可控性，并使用全局状态编码器确保不同视角之间的观察一致性。实验结果表明，MultiWorld在视频质量、动作跟随能力和多视角一致性方面优于现有基线。

papers.title: EasyVideoR1: Easier RL for Video Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract EasyVideoR1 presents an efficient reinforcement learning framework for video understanding that improves training throughput, supports diverse video tasks, and enables joint image-video training with comprehensive evaluation across multiple benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) has demonstrated remarkable effectiveness in improving the reasoning capabilities of large language models. As models evolve into natively multimodal architectures, extending RLVR to video understanding becomes increasingly important yet remains largely unexplored, due to the diversity of video task types, the computational overhead of repeatedly decoding and preprocessing high-dimensional visual inputs, and the difficulty of reproducible evaluation across numerous sensitive hyperparameters. Existing open-source RL training frameworks provide solid infrastructure for text and image scenarios but lack systematic optimizations tailored for video modality. In this work, we present EasyVideoR1, a complete and efficient reinforcement learning framework specifically designed for training large vision-language models on video understanding tasks. EasyVideoR1 makes the following contributions: (1) a full video RL training pipeline with offline preprocessing and tensor caching that eliminates redundant video decoding and yields a 1.47 times throughput improvement; (2) a comprehensive, task-aware reward system covering 11 distinct video and image problem types with unified routing and modular extension; (3) a mixed offline-online data training paradigm that combines curated high-quality trajectories with on-policy exploration, benefiting the learning of more challenging tasks; (4) joint image-video training with independently configurable pixel budgets, allowing the two modalities to mutually reinforce each other; and (5) an asynchronous multi-benchmark evaluation framework covering 22 mainstream video understanding benchmarks, with reproduced accuracy closely aligned with officially reported scores.
affiliations: Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China
School of Cyber Security, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China

ru.title: Эффективное обучение с подкреплением для понимания видео с оптимизированной предварительной обработкой
ru.desc: EasyVideoR1 — это框架для обучения больших видео-языковых моделей с использованием обучения с подкреплением на основе проверяемых вознаграждений. Система включает оптимизированный конвейер предварительной обработки видео с кешированием тензоров, что повышает пропускную способность в 1.47 раза и устраняет избыточное декодирование. Фреймворк предоставляет универсальную систему вознаграждений для 11 типов видео и изображений, а также поддерживает смешанное обучение на офлайн-онлайн данных для улучшения обучения на сложных задачах. Работа включает совместное обучение изображений и видео с независимыми бюджетами пикселей и комплексную оценку на 22 основных бенчмарках понимания видео.

en.title: Revolutionizing Video Understanding with Efficient RL
en.desc: EasyVideoR1 is a novel reinforcement learning framework designed to enhance video understanding by improving training efficiency and supporting various video tasks. It introduces a comprehensive video RL training pipeline that optimizes preprocessing and caching, resulting in a significant increase in throughput. The framework also features a task-aware reward system that accommodates multiple video and image problem types, facilitating better learning outcomes. Additionally, it allows for joint training of image and video data, promoting synergy between the two modalities while providing robust evaluation across numerous benchmarks.

zh.title: 高效视频理解的强化学习框架
zh.desc: EasyVideoR1是一个高效的强化学习框架，专门用于视频理解任务。它通过离线预处理和张量缓存，消除了冗余的视频解码，从而提高了训练的吞吐量。该框架还提供了一个全面的奖励系统，涵盖11种不同的视频和图像问题类型，并支持联合图像-视频训练。通过这种方式，EasyVideoR1能够有效地提升大规模视觉-语言模型在视频理解方面的性能。

papers.title: GFT: From Imitation to Reward Fine-Tuning with Unbiased Group Advantages and Dynamic Coefficient Rectification
papers.abstract: Abstract Group Fine-Tuning addresses limitations in supervised fine-tuning by using diverse response groups and adaptive weight bounding to improve training stability and efficiency.  AI-generated summary Large language models are typically post-trained using supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL), yet effectively unifying efficient knowledge injection with robust generalization remains challenging. In this work, we provide a training-dynamics analysis showing that SFT can be interpreted as a special case of policy gradient optimization with an extremely sparse implicit reward and unstable inverse-probability weighting, which together lead to single-path dependency, entropy collapse, and gradient explosion. Motivated by this diagnosis, we propose Group Fine-Tuning (GFT), a unified post-training framework that addresses these intrinsic limitations through two mechanisms: Group Advantage Learning, which constructs diverse response groups and derives normalized contrastive supervision to alleviate reward sparsity, and Dynamic Coefficient Rectification, which adaptively bounds inverse-probability weights to stabilize optimization while preserving efficient knowledge injection. Experiments demonstrate that GFT consistently surpasses SFT-based methods and yields policies that integrate more smoothly with subsequent RL training.
affiliations: School of Software Technolog, Zhejiang University

ru.title: Групповое обучение для стабильной настройки языковых моделей
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Group Fine-Tuning (GFT), который улучшает процесс обучения большие языковые модели через анализ динамики тренировки. Авторы показывают, что стандартный supervised fine-tuning можно интерпретировать как частный случай policy gradient оптимизации с разреженной наградой и нестабильным взвешиванием. Метод использует две ключевые компоненты: Group Advantage Learning для создания разнообразных групп ответов с контрастивным обучением, и Dynamic Coefficient Rectification для адаптивной стабилизации весов. Эксперименты демонстрируют, что GFT превосходит стандартные методы SFT и лучше интегрируется с последующим обучением с подкреплением.

en.title: Enhancing Fine-Tuning with Group Dynamics
en.desc: This paper introduces Group Fine-Tuning (GFT) as a solution to the challenges faced in supervised fine-tuning (SFT) of large language models. GFT enhances training stability and efficiency by utilizing diverse response groups and adaptive weight bounding techniques. The authors analyze the limitations of SFT, such as sparse rewards and unstable optimization, and propose mechanisms to overcome these issues. Experimental results show that GFT outperforms traditional SFT methods and improves the integration of models with reinforcement learning.

zh.title: 群体微调：提升训练稳定性与效率的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了群体微调（Group Fine-Tuning, GFT）方法，以解决监督微调（SFT）中的一些局限性。GFT通过构建多样化的响应组和自适应权重限制，来提高训练的稳定性和效率。研究表明，SFT可以视为一种特殊的策略梯度优化，但存在稀疏奖励和不稳定的逆概率加权问题。实验结果显示，GFT在性能上优于基于SFT的方法，并能更好地与后续的强化学习训练结合。

papers.title: WebCompass: Towards Multimodal Web Coding Evaluation for Code Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract WebCompass evaluates web development capabilities through diverse input modalities and task types, using automated evaluation methods that simulate real-world coding workflows.  AI-generated summary Large language models are rapidly evolving into interactive coding agents capable of end-to-end web coding, yet existing benchmarks evaluate only narrow slices of this capability, typically text-conditioned generation with static-correctness metrics, leaving visual fidelity, interaction quality, and codebase-level reasoning largely unmeasured. We introduce WebCompass, a multimodal benchmark that provides unified lifecycle evaluation of web engineering capability. Recognizing that real-world web coding is an iterative cycle of generation, editing, and repair, WebCompass spans three input modalities (text, image, video) and three task types (generation, editing, repair), yielding seven task categories that mirror professional workflows. Through a multi-stage, human-in-the-loop pipeline, we curate instances covering 15 generation domains, 16 editing operation types, and 11 repair defect types, each annotated at Easy/Medium/Hard levels. For evaluation, we adopt a checklist-guided LLM-as-a-Judge protocol for editing and repair, and propose a novel Agent-as-a-Judge paradigm for generation that autonomously executes generated websites in a real browser, explores interactive behaviors via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and iteratively synthesizes targeted test cases, closely approximating human acceptance testing. We evaluate representative closed-source and open-source models and observe that: (1) closed-source models remain substantially stronger and more balanced; (2) editing and repair exhibit distinct difficulty profiles, with repair preserving interactivity better but remaining execution-challenging; (3) aesthetics is the most persistent bottleneck, especially for open-source models; and (4) framework choice materially affects outcomes, with Vue consistently challenging while React and Vanilla/HTML perform more strongly depending on task type.

ru.title: Полный цикл оценки веб-кодирования через мультимодальный бенчмарк
ru.desc: WebCompass — это мультимодальный бенчмарк для комплексной оценки способностей больших языковых моделей в веб-разработке. Бенчмарк охватывает три типа входных данных (текст, изображение, видео) и три типа задач (генерация, редактирование, исправление кода), отражая реальные рабочие процессы разработчиков. Для оценки применяются инновационные методы, включая протокол LLM-as-a-Judge и новую парадигму Agent-as-a-Judge, которая автоматически выполняет сгенерированные веб-сайты в реальном браузере и проводит интерактивное тестирование. Исследование показало, что закрытые модели превосходят открытые, эстетика остаётся главным вызовом, а выбор фреймворка существенно влияет на результаты.

en.title: WebCompass: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Web Development Skills
en.desc: WebCompass is a new benchmark designed to evaluate web development skills using various input types like text, images, and videos. It focuses on real-world coding tasks, including generating, editing, and repairing code, which reflects how professionals actually work. The evaluation process uses advanced methods, such as having AI act as a judge to test the generated websites in real browsers. The study finds that closed-source models perform better overall, while aesthetics and framework choice significantly impact the results.

zh.title: WebCompass：全面评估网页开发能力的多模态基准
zh.desc: WebCompass 是一个多模态基准，用于评估网页开发能力，涵盖文本、图像和视频三种输入方式，以及生成、编辑和修复三种任务类型。该基准模拟真实的编码工作流程，提供统一的生命周期评估，帮助更全面地理解 AI 在网页编码中的表现。通过多阶段的人机协作流程，WebCompass 收集了涵盖多种生成领域和操作类型的实例，并对其进行难度分级。评估方法包括使用 LLM 作为评判者的检查表指导和新颖的 Agent 作为评判者的范式，能够在真实浏览器中执行生成的网站，模拟人类接受测试。

papers.title: SkillFlow:Benchmarking Lifelong Skill Discovery and Evolution for Autonomous Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract SkillFlow presents a benchmark for evaluating autonomous agents' ability to discover, repair, and maintain skills over time through a structured lifelong learning protocol.  AI-generated summary As the capability frontier of autonomous agents continues to expand, they are increasingly able to complete specialized tasks through plug-and-play external skills. Yet current benchmarks mostly test whether models can use provided skills, leaving open whether they can discover skills from experience, repair them after failure, and maintain a coherent library over time. We introduce SkillFlow, a benchmark of 166 tasks across 20 families in which task construction within each family follows a Domain-Agnostic Execution Flow (DAEF) that defines an agent workflow framework, allowing these tasks to share a consistent workflow. Agents are evaluated under an Agentic Lifelong Learning protocol in which they begin without skills, solve tasks sequentially within each family, externalize lessons through trajectory- and rubric-driven skill patches, and carry the updated library forward. Experiments reveal a substantial capability gap. For Claude Opus 4.6, lifelong skill evolution improves task success from 62.65% to 71.08% (+8.43 points). However, high skill usage does not necessarily imply high utility: Kimi K2.5 gains only +0.60 points despite 66.87% skill usage, while Qwen-Coder-Next reaches only a 44.58% task completion rate and still regresses relative to the vanilla setting. SkillFlow contributes a structured testbed for this direction and an in-depth empirical analysis of skill discovery, patching, transfer, and their failure modes under lifelong evaluation.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Непрерывное открытие и эволюция навыков в автономных агентах
ru.desc: SkillFlow — это бенчмарк для оценки способности автономных агентов обнаруживать, исправлять и поддерживать навыки в процессе непрерывного обучения. В работе представлена серия из 166 задач, организованных в 20 семействах с единой структурой выполнения (DAEF), что позволяет агентам использовать согласованный рабочий процесс. Агенты оцениваются по протоколу пожизненного обучения, начиная без навыков и постепенно накапливая их библиотеку через анализ траекторий выполнения и обратной связи. Эксперименты показывают значительный разрыв в возможностях: эволюция навыков повышает успешность выполнения задач, но корреляция между использованием навыков и их полезностью не всегда очевидна.

en.title: Empowering Agents: Discover, Repair, and Maintain Skills with SkillFlow
en.desc: SkillFlow is a benchmark designed to assess how well autonomous agents can learn and manage skills over time. It focuses on three key abilities: discovering new skills from their experiences, repairing skills when they fail, and maintaining a library of skills consistently. The benchmark includes 166 tasks across 20 families, structured to allow agents to learn in a coherent workflow. Results show that while some agents improve their task success rates with lifelong learning, others demonstrate that high skill usage does not always correlate with better performance.

zh.title: SkillFlow：自主智能体技能演进的新基准
zh.desc: SkillFlow是一个基准测试，用于评估自主智能体在长期学习过程中发现、修复和维护技能的能力。该基准包含166个任务，分为20个任务家族，采用领域无关的执行流程（DAEF）来定义智能体的工作流程。智能体在没有技能的情况下开始，依次解决每个家族中的任务，并通过轨迹和评分驱动的技能补丁外化经验。实验结果显示，尽管技能使用率高，但并不一定意味着高效能，SkillFlow为技能发现和维护提供了结构化的测试平台。

papers.title: Crowded in B-Space: Calibrating Shared Directions for LoRA Merging
papers.abstract: Abstract LoRA adapter merging performance can be improved by separately calibrating the output-side matrix B to reduce interference from shared directions while preserving task-specific information.  AI-generated summary Merging separately trained LoRA adapters is a practical alternative to joint multi-task training, but it often hurts performance. Existing methods usually treat the LoRA update ΔW = BA as a single object and do not distinguish the two LoRA matrices. We show that the main source of LoRA merge interference comes from the output-side matrix B. Across tasks, B repeatedly uses a small set of shared directions, while A remains much more task-specific. As a result, the merged adapter overemphasizes these shared directions, and task-specific information is lost. We propose Pico (Pre-merge interference calibration in output-space), a data-free method that calibrates B before merge by downscaling over-shared directions and then rescaling the merged update. Pico plugs directly into existing merging methods such as Task Arithmetic, TIES, and TSV-M. Across eight different benchmarks from math, coding, finance, and medical domains, Pico improves average accuracy by 3.4-8.3 points over the corresponding base method and achieves the best overall average performance. Pico also enables merged adapters to outperform the LoRA trained with all task data. These results show that LoRA merging works better when the two LoRA matrices are treated separately.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Раздельная калибровка матриц LoRA для улучшения слияния адаптеров
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема слияния LoRA адаптеров, обученных на разных задачах. Авторы обнаружили, что основной источник помех при слиянии — это выходная матрица B, которая переиспользует общие направления во всех задачах, в то время как матрица A остаётся специфичной для конкретных задач. Предложен метод Pico, который калибрует матрицу B перед слиянием, подавляя переполненные общие направления и масштабируя объединённое обновление. Метод показывает улучшение точности на 3.4-8.3 процентных пункта и совместим с существующими техниками слияния адаптеров.

en.title: Enhancing LoRA Merging with Pico Calibration
en.desc: This paper discusses improving the performance of merging LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) adapters by separately calibrating the output-side matrix B. The authors identify that interference from shared directions in B can lead to a loss of task-specific information when merging. They introduce a method called Pico, which calibrates B by downscaling over-shared directions before merging, thus enhancing the final output. The results demonstrate that this approach significantly boosts accuracy across various tasks, outperforming traditional merging methods and even full task data training.

zh.title: 优化LoRA适配器合并，提升任务性能
zh.desc: 本文探讨了LoRA适配器合并的性能提升方法。研究发现，合并时输出侧矩阵B的干扰是主要问题，导致任务特定信息的丢失。我们提出了一种名为Pico的方法，通过在合并前对B进行校准，减少共享方向的干扰，从而保留任务特定信息。实验结果表明，Pico在多个领域的基准测试中显著提高了准确率，证明了分别处理LoRA矩阵的有效性。

papers.title: When Can LLMs Learn to Reason with Weak Supervision?
papers.abstract: Abstract Research reveals that model generalization in reasoning tasks under weak supervision depends on reward saturation dynamics and reasoning faithfulness, with supervised fine-tuning on explicit traces being crucial for successful adaptation.  AI-generated summary Large language models have achieved significant reasoning improvements through reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). Yet as model capabilities grow, constructing high-quality reward signals becomes increasingly difficult, making it essential to understand when RLVR can succeed under weaker forms of supervision. We conduct a systematic empirical study across diverse model families and reasoning domains under three weak supervision settings: scarce data, noisy rewards, and self-supervised proxy rewards. We find that generalization is governed by training reward saturation dynamics: models that generalize exhibit a prolonged pre-saturation phase during which training reward and downstream performance climb together, while models that saturate rapidly memorize rather than learn. We identify reasoning faithfulness, defined as the extent to which intermediate steps logically support the final answer, as the pre-RL property that predicts which regime a model falls into, while output diversity alone is uninformative. Motivated by these findings, we disentangle the contributions of continual pre-training and supervised fine-tuning, finding that SFT on explicit reasoning traces is necessary for generalization under weak supervision, while continual pre-training on domain data amplifies the effect. Applied together to Llama3.2-3B-Base, these interventions enable generalization across all three settings where the base model previously failed.

ru.title: Верность рассуждений и насыщение вознаграждения как ключи к обучению под слабым контролем
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что обобщение языковых моделей при решении логических задач под слабым контролем зависит от динамики насыщения вознаграждения и верности рассуждений. Авторы провели систематическое эмпирическое исследование на нескольких семействах моделей под тремя условиями слабого контроля: дефицит данных, зашумленные награды и самоконтролируемые прокси-награды. Они обнаружили, что модели, которые обобщают хорошо, показывают продолжительную фазу до насыщения, где улучшение награды коррелирует с качеством решений, а верность промежуточных шагов рассуждений предсказывает успех обучения. Оказалось, что обучение с учителем на явных цепочках рассуждений критически важно для обобщения при слабом контроле, и это позволило получить генерализацию на базовой модели Llama3.2-3B-B базе.

en.title: Unlocking Generalization in Weakly Supervised Reasoning
en.desc: This paper investigates how well machine learning models can generalize their reasoning abilities when trained under weak supervision. It highlights the importance of reward saturation dynamics, where models that learn effectively show a gradual increase in performance, while those that memorize quickly fail to generalize. The study also emphasizes the role of reasoning faithfulness, which measures how well intermediate reasoning steps support the final answer, as a predictor of model success. Finally, it demonstrates that supervised fine-tuning on explicit reasoning examples is crucial for achieving generalization, especially when combined with continual pre-training on relevant data.

zh.title: 弱监督下的推理泛化与奖励动态
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了在弱监督下，模型在推理任务中的泛化能力与奖励饱和动态和推理可信度之间的关系。研究发现，模型的泛化能力与训练奖励饱和动态密切相关，表现出较长的预饱和阶段的模型能够更好地学习，而快速饱和的模型则倾向于记忆而非学习。推理可信度被定义为中间步骤在多大程度上逻辑支持最终答案，是预测模型表现的关键属性。通过对Llama3.2-3B-Base模型的实验，发现显式推理轨迹的监督微调对于在弱监督下的泛化是必要的，而在领域数据上的持续预训练则增强了这一效果。

papers.title: ClawEnvKit: Automatic Environment Generation for Claw-Like Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract An automated pipeline generates diverse, verified environments for claw-like agents from natural language descriptions, enabling large-scale benchmark construction and continuous evaluation.  AI-generated summary Constructing environments for training and evaluating claw-like agents remains a manual, human-intensive process that does not scale. We argue that what is needed is not just a dataset, but an automated pipeline capable of generating diverse, verified environments on demand. To this end, we introduce ClawEnvKit, an autonomous generation pipeline that instantiates this formalism from natural language descriptions. The pipeline comprises three modules: (1) a parser that extracts structured generation parameters from natural language input; (2) a generator that produces the task specification, tool interface, and scoring configuration; and (3) a validator that enforces feasibility, diversity, structural validity, and internal consistency across the generated environments. Using ClawEnvKit, we construct Auto-ClawEval, the first large-scale benchmark for claw-like agents, comprising 1,040 environments across 24 categories. Empirically, Auto-ClawEval matches or exceeds human-curated environments on coherence and clarity at 13,800x lower cost. Evaluated across 4 model families and 8 agent harness frameworks, we find that harness engineering boosts performance by up to 15.7 percentage points over a bare ReAct baseline, completion remains the primary axis of variation with no model saturating the benchmark, and automated generation enables evaluation at a scale previously infeasible. Beyond static benchmarking, ClawEnvKit enables live evaluation: users describe a desired capability in natural language and obtain a verified environment on demand, turning evaluation into a continuous, user-driven process. The same mechanism serves as an on-demand training environment generator, producing task distributions that adapt to an agent's current weaknesses rather than being bounded by existing user logs.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence

ru.title: Автоматическая генерация окружений для обучения и оценки агентов-манипуляторов из естественного языка
ru.desc: В работе представлен ClawEnvKit — автоматизированный конвейер для генерации разнообразных окружений для агентов-манипуляторов из описаний на естественном языке. Система состоит из трёх модулей: парсера для извлечения параметров, генератора спецификаций задач и валидатора для проверки корректности. На основе ClawEnvKit создан Auto-ClawEval — первый крупномасштабный бенчмарк с 1040 окружениями, генерируемыми в 13800 раз дешевле, чем ручная разметка. Система позволяет не только масштабируемо оценивать агентов, но и выполнять непрерывное обучение, адаптируя распределение задач к слабым местам конкретного агента.

en.title: Automating Environment Generation for Claw-Like Agents
en.desc: This paper presents ClawEnvKit, an automated pipeline that generates diverse and verified environments for claw-like agents based on natural language descriptions. The pipeline consists of three main components: a parser to extract parameters, a generator to create task specifications, and a validator to ensure the environments are feasible and diverse. By using ClawEnvKit, researchers can construct the Auto-ClawEval benchmark, which includes over 1,000 environments and significantly reduces the cost of environment creation while maintaining high quality. This system not only facilitates large-scale evaluation but also allows for continuous, user-driven training and assessment of agents based on their specific needs.

zh.title: 自动化生成爪形智能体环境的革命
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了一种名为ClawEnvKit的自动化生成管道，能够根据自然语言描述生成多样化且经过验证的环境，以训练和评估爪形智能体。该管道包含三个模块：解析器、生成器和验证器，分别负责提取参数、生成任务规范和验证环境的有效性。通过ClawEnvKit，我们构建了Auto-ClawEval，这是第一个大规模的爪形智能体基准，包含1040个环境，成本比人工策划低13800倍。该系统不仅支持静态基准测试，还允许用户实时描述需求，生成适应性训练环境，推动评估过程的持续进行。

papers.title: Revisiting a Pain in the Neck: A Semantic Reasoning Benchmark for Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract SemanticQA evaluates language models on semantic phrase processing tasks, revealing significant performance variations in reasoning and comprehension across different phrase types and model architectures.  AI-generated summary We present SemanticQA, an evaluation suite designed to assess language models (LMs) in semantic phrase processing tasks. The benchmark consolidates existing multiword expression (MwE) resources and reorganizes them into a unified testbed. It covers both general lexical phenomena, such as lexical collocations, and three fine-grained categories: idiomatic expressions, noun compounds, and verbal constructions. Through SemanticQA, we assess LMs of diverse architectures and scales in extraction, classification, and interpretation tasks, as well as sequential task compositions. We reveal substantial performance variation, particularly on tasks requiring semantic reasoning, highlighting differences in reasoning efficacy and semantic understanding of LMs, providing insights for pushing LMs with stronger comprehension on non-trivial semantic phrases. The evaluation harness and data of SemanticQA are available at https://github.com/jacklanda/SemanticQA.
affiliations: State Key Laboratory of General Artificial Intelligence, BIGAI
University of Science and Technology Beijing

ru.title: Оценка семантического понимания языковых моделей через сложные фразовые конструкции
ru.desc: SemanticQA — это набор для оценки языковых моделей на задачах обработки семантических фраз, включающий многословные выражения, идиоматические конструкции и составные существительные. Авторы тестируют модели различных архитектур на задачах извлечения, классификации и интерпретации семантических единиц. Исследование выявляет значительные различия в производительности моделей, особенно на задачах, требующих семантического рассуждения. Результаты показывают, что существующие языковые модели испытывают трудности с пониманием нетривиальных семантических фраз.

en.title: Unlocking Language Models: Evaluating Semantic Understanding
en.desc: SemanticQA is a benchmark designed to evaluate language models on their ability to process semantic phrases. It organizes various multiword expression resources into a single testbed, focusing on both general lexical phenomena and specific categories like idiomatic expressions and noun compounds. The evaluation reveals significant differences in how well different models perform on tasks that require semantic reasoning and comprehension. This insight helps identify areas where language models can be improved for better understanding of complex semantic phrases.

zh.title: 评估语言模型的语义理解能力
zh.desc: SemanticQA是一个评估语言模型在语义短语处理任务上的工具。它整合了现有的多词表达资源，并将其重新组织成一个统一的测试平台。该基准涵盖了通用的词汇现象和三种细分类别，包括习语、名词复合词和动词结构。通过SemanticQA，我们评估了不同架构和规模的语言模型在提取、分类和解释任务中的表现，揭示了在语义推理任务上显著的性能差异。

papers.title: OmniScript: Towards Audio-Visual Script Generation for Long-Form Cinematic Video
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel video-to-script task is introduced along with OmniScript, an 8B-parameter omni-modal language model trained through progressive pipeline techniques for long-form narrative comprehension and temporal localization.  AI-generated summary Current multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in short-form video understanding, yet translating long-form cinematic videos into detailed, temporally grounded scripts remains a significant challenge. This paper introduces the novel video-to-script (V2S) task, aiming to generate hierarchical, scene-by-scene scripts encompassing character actions, dialogues, expressions, and audio cues. To facilitate this, we construct a first-of-its-kind human-annotated benchmark and propose a temporally-aware hierarchical evaluation framework. Furthermore, we present OmniScript, an 8B-parameter omni-modal (audio-visual) language model tailored for long-form narrative comprehension. OmniScript is trained via a progressive pipeline that leverages chain-of-thought supervised fine-tuning for plot and character reasoning, followed by reinforcement learning using temporally segmented rewards. Extensive experiments demonstrate that despite its parameter efficiency, OmniScript significantly outperforms larger open-source models and achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art proprietary models, including Gemini 3-Pro, in both temporal localization and multi-field semantic accuracy.

ru.title: От видео к сценарию: омниомодальная модель для понимания длинных кинематографических произведений
ru.desc: В работе предложена новая задача видео-в-сценарий (V2S) и модель OmniScript - омниомодальная языковая модель с 8 миллиардами параметров, которая понимает длинные видеоконтенты и локализует события во времени. Модель обучается с использованием прогрессивного конвейера, включающего supervised fine-tuning с цепочками рассуждений для анализа сюжета и персонажей, а затем reinforcement learning с временными сегментированными наградами. Авторы создали первый размеченный вручную бенчмарк для этой задачи и разработали иерархическую систему оценки, учитывающую временную локализацию. Несмотря на относительную компактность, OmniScript превосходит более крупные открытые модели и показывает результаты, сравнимые с передовыми проприетарными системами типа Gemini 3-Pro.

en.title: Transforming Videos into Detailed Scripts with OmniScript
en.desc: This paper introduces a new task called video-to-script (V2S), which focuses on converting long cinematic videos into detailed scripts that include character actions, dialogues, and audio cues. To support this task, the authors developed OmniScript, an 8 billion parameter omni-modal language model designed for understanding complex narratives. The model is trained using a progressive pipeline that combines supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning to enhance its ability to reason about plots and characters. The results show that OmniScript outperforms larger models and competes well with leading proprietary models in terms of accuracy and temporal understanding.

zh.title: 视频到脚本：长篇叙事的新突破
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新的视频到脚本（V2S）任务，旨在将长篇电影视频转换为详细的分场景脚本，涵盖角色动作、对话、表情和音频提示。为此，研究者构建了一个首个人工标注的基准数据集，并提出了一种时序感知的分层评估框架。文章还介绍了OmniScript，这是一种具有8亿参数的全模态语言模型，专门用于长篇叙事理解。通过逐步管道训练，OmniScript在情节和角色推理方面表现出色，并在时序定位和多领域语义准确性上超越了许多大型开源模型。

papers.title: Meta-learning In-Context Enables Training-Free Cross Subject Brain Decoding
papers.abstract: Abstract A meta-optimized approach enables generalizable semantic visual decoding from fMRI by rapidly inferring unique neural encoding patterns from few image-brain examples without fine-tuning across subjects and scanners.  AI-generated summary Visual decoding from brain signals is a key challenge at the intersection of computer vision and neuroscience, requiring methods that bridge neural representations and computational models of vision. A field-wide goal is to achieve generalizable, cross-subject models. A major obstacle towards this goal is the substantial variability in neural representations across individuals, which has so far required training bespoke models or fine-tuning separately for each subject. To address this challenge, we introduce a meta-optimized approach for semantic visual decoding from fMRI that generalizes to novel subjects without any fine-tuning. By simply conditioning on a small set of image-brain activation examples from the new individual, our model rapidly infers their unique neural encoding patterns to facilitate robust and efficient visual decoding. Our approach is explicitly optimized for in-context learning of the new subject's encoding model and performs decoding by hierarchical inference, inverting the encoder. First, for multiple brain regions, we estimate the per-voxel visual response encoder parameters by constructing a context over multiple stimuli and responses. Second, we construct a context consisting of encoder parameters and response values over multiple voxels to perform aggregated functional inversion. We demonstrate strong cross-subject and cross-scanner generalization across diverse visual backbones without retraining or fine-tuning. Moreover, our approach requires neither anatomical alignment nor stimulus overlap. This work is a critical step towards a generalizable foundation model for non-invasive brain decoding.

ru.title: Универсальное декодирование мозга через мета-обучение без переобучения
ru.desc: Авторы предлагают мета-оптимизированный подход для семантического декодирования зрительной информации из сигналов fMRI, который обобщается на новых субъектов без дополнительной настройки. Метод использует контекстное обучение, быстро выводя уникальные паттерны нейронного кодирования отдельного человека из небольшого набора примеров изображение-активность мозга. Подход выполняет декодирование через иерархическую инверсию энкодера, последовательно восстанавливая параметры визуальной реакции для отдельных вокселей и затем для их совокупности. Демонстрируется сильное кросс-субъектное и кросс-сканерное обобщение без переобучения, без анатомической выравнивания и требования совпадения стимулов.

en.title: Meta-Optimized Visual Decoding: Generalizing Brain Insights Without Fine-Tuning
en.desc: This paper presents a meta-optimized method for semantic visual decoding from fMRI data, allowing for the inference of unique neural encoding patterns with minimal examples. The approach addresses the challenge of variability in neural representations across individuals, eliminating the need for fine-tuning or bespoke models for each subject. By conditioning on a small set of image-brain activation examples, the model efficiently learns the new subject's encoding patterns through hierarchical inference. This method demonstrates strong generalization capabilities across different subjects and scanners, paving the way for a more robust foundation in non-invasive brain decoding.

zh.title: 无须微调的跨受试者视觉解码新方法
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种元优化的方法，可以从fMRI数据中进行语义视觉解码，能够在没有针对每个受试者和扫描仪进行微调的情况下，快速推断出独特的神经编码模式。该方法通过少量的图像-大脑激活示例，快速推断新个体的神经编码模式，从而实现稳健和高效的视觉解码。我们的方法专门优化了新受试者编码模型的上下文学习，并通过分层推理进行解码。我们的研究在不同的视觉基础上展示了强大的跨受试者和跨扫描仪的泛化能力，且不需要解剖对齐或刺激重叠。

papers.title: Stratagem: Learning Transferable Reasoning via Trajectory-Modulated Game Self-Play
papers.abstract: Abstract STRATAGEM addresses limitations in reasoning transfer for language models by using a reasoning transferability coefficient and evolution reward to promote abstract, domain-agnostic patterns over game-specific heuristics.  AI-generated summary Games offer a compelling paradigm for developing general reasoning capabilities in language models, as they naturally demand strategic planning, probabilistic inference, and adaptive decision-making. However, existing self-play approaches rely solely on terminal game outcomes, providing no mechanism to distinguish transferable reasoning patterns from game-specific heuristics. We present STRATAGEM, which addresses two fundamental barriers to reasoning transfer: domain specificity, where learned patterns remain anchored in game semantics, and contextual stasis, where static game contexts fail to cultivate progressive reasoning. STRATAGEM selectively reinforces trajectories exhibiting abstract, domain-agnostic reasoning through a Reasoning Transferability Coefficient, while incentivizing adaptive reasoning development via a Reasoning Evolution Reward. Experiments across mathematical reasoning, general reasoning, and code generation benchmarks demonstrate substantial improvements, with particularly strong gains on competition-level mathematics where multi-step reasoning is critical. Ablation studies and human evaluation confirm that both components contribute to transferable reasoning.

ru.title: От игровых стратегий к универсальному мышлению: учим модели переносить рассуждения
ru.desc: STRATAGEM — это метод обучения языковых моделей, который решает проблему передачи рассуждений между разными задачами. Авторы предложили использовать коэффициент передаваемости рассуждений и эволюционную награду для подкрепления абстрактных, независимых от домена паттернов вместо специфичных для игры эвристик. Метод применяется к самоигре языковых моделей и позволяет развивать многошаговое логическое мышление, которое хорошо переносится на математические задачи и генерацию кода. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительные улучшения на бенчмарках, особенно на задачах олимпиадной математики, где требуется глубокое рассуждение.

en.title: Enhancing Reasoning Transfer in Language Models with STRATAGEM
en.desc: STRATAGEM is a method designed to improve how language models transfer reasoning skills across different tasks. It introduces a Reasoning Transferability Coefficient to identify and promote reasoning patterns that are not tied to specific games. Additionally, it uses a Reasoning Evolution Reward to encourage models to adapt their reasoning strategies over time. Experiments show that STRATAGEM significantly enhances performance in various reasoning tasks, especially in complex scenarios requiring multi-step thinking.

zh.title: 提升推理转移能力的STRATAGEM
zh.desc: STRATAGEM 解决了语言模型在推理转移方面的局限性，通过使用推理转移系数和演化奖励来促进抽象的、领域无关的模式，而不是特定于游戏的启发式方法。该方法强调在游戏中发展一般推理能力的重要性，因为游戏自然需要战略规划、概率推理和适应性决策。STRATAGEM 通过选择性强化展示抽象推理的轨迹，并激励适应性推理的发展，克服了领域特异性和上下文静态性这两个基本障碍。实验结果表明，该方法在数学推理、一般推理和代码生成基准测试中显著提高了性能，尤其在多步骤推理至关重要的竞争级数学中表现尤为突出。

papers.title: Precise Debugging Benchmark: Is Your Model Debugging or Regenerating?
papers.abstract: Abstract Frontier LLMs demonstrate high test pass rates but poor precision in debugging tasks, indicating a gap between functional correctness and precise fault localization.  AI-generated summary Unlike code completion, debugging requires localizing faults and applying targeted edits. We observe that frontier LLMs often regenerate correct but over-edited solutions during debugging. To evaluate how far LLMs are from precise debugging, we introduce the Precise Debugging Benchmark (PDB) framework, which automatically converts any coding dataset into a debugging benchmark with precision-aware evaluation. PDB generates buggy programs by synthesizing verified atomic bugs and composing them into multi-bug programs. We define two novel metrics, edit-level precision and bug-level recall, which measures how many necessary edits are made and how many bugs are resolved. We release two evaluation benchmarks: PDB-Single-Hard on single-line bugs, and PDB-Multi on multi-line bugs. Experiments show that frontier models, such as GPT-5.1-Codex and DeepSeek-V3.2-Thinking, achieve unit-test pass rates above 76% but exhibit precision below 45%, even when explicitly instructed to perform minimal debugging. Finally, we show that iterative and agentic debugging strategies do not substantially improve precision or recall, highlighting the need to rethink post-training pipelines for coding models.

ru.title: Точная отладка кода: почему LLM вносят лишние правки
ru.desc: Статья показывает, что современные большие языковые модели хорошо решают задачи написания кода, но плохо справляются с отладкой, так как они часто вносят лишние правки вместо целевого исправления ошибок. Авторы предлагают фреймворк Precise Debugging Benchmark (PDB), который автоматически преобразует любой набор данных с кодом в бенчмарк для оценки точности отладки. Они вводят новые метрики — точность на уровне правок и полнота на уровне ошибок, которые оценивают, насколько минимальны внесённые изменения и сколько ошибок действительно исправлено. Эксперименты показывают, что даже передовые модели достигают точности ниже 45%, и даже итеративные стратегии отладки не улучшают результаты существенно.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Precision in Debugging with PDB
en.desc: This paper addresses the limitations of frontier large language models (LLMs) in debugging tasks, where they often achieve high test pass rates but lack precision in identifying and fixing code errors. The authors introduce the Precise Debugging Benchmark (PDB), a framework that transforms coding datasets into debugging benchmarks with a focus on precision-aware evaluation. PDB generates buggy programs by creating verified atomic bugs and combining them into more complex multi-bug scenarios. The study reveals that even advanced models struggle with precision in debugging, prompting a reevaluation of how coding models are trained and fine-tuned for better performance.

zh.title: 精确调试：填补功能与精度的鸿沟
zh.desc: 前沿的大型语言模型（LLMs）在测试通过率上表现良好，但在调试任务中精确度较低，显示出功能正确性与精确故障定位之间的差距。调试不仅需要定位故障，还需要进行针对性的编辑，而前沿LLMs在调试时常常生成正确但过度编辑的解决方案。为评估LLMs在精确调试方面的表现，我们引入了精确调试基准（PDB）框架，该框架能够将任何编码数据集自动转换为具有精确度评估的调试基准。我们定义了两个新指标，编辑级精确度和故障级召回率，以衡量必要编辑的数量和解决的故障数量，并发布了两个评估基准：PDB-Single-Hard和PDB-Multi。

papers.title: MedConclusion: A Benchmark for Biomedical Conclusion Generation from Structured Abstracts
papers.abstract: Abstract A large-scale dataset of 5.7 million PubMed structured abstracts is introduced for biomedical conclusion generation, enabling evaluation of large language models' ability to reason from structured scientific evidence.  AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) are widely explored for reasoning-intensive research tasks, yet resources for testing whether they can infer scientific conclusions from structured biomedical evidence remain limited. We introduce MedConclusion, a large-scale dataset of 5.7M PubMed structured abstracts for biomedical conclusion generation. Each instance pairs the non-conclusion sections of an abstract with the original author-written conclusion, providing naturally occurring supervision for evidence-to-conclusion reasoning. MedConclusion also includes journal-level metadata such as biomedical category and SJR, enabling subgroup analysis across biomedical domains. As an initial study, we evaluate diverse LLMs under conclusion and summary prompting settings and score outputs with both reference-based metrics and LLM-as-a-judge. We find that conclusion writing is behaviorally distinct from summary writing, strong models remain closely clustered under current automatic metrics, and judge identity can substantially shift absolute scores. MedConclusion provides a reusable data resource for studying scientific evidence-to-conclusion reasoning. Our code and data are available at: https://github.com/Harvard-AI-and-Robotics-Lab/MedConclusion.
affiliations: Harvard AI and Robotics Lab, Harvard Medical School
Kempner Institute for the Study of Natural and Artificial Intelligence, Harvard University

ru.title: От научных фактов к выводам: датасет для обучения логическому рассуждению моделей
ru.desc: Представлен новый датасет MedConclusion, содержащий 5.7 миллионов структурированных аннотаций из PubMed для задачи генерации выводов в биомедицинской области. Датасет позволяет оценивать способность больших языковых моделей выполнять рассуждения на основе структурированных научных доказательств. В исследовании авторы проводят экспериментальное сравнение различных LLM под разными настройками промптов и оценивают результаты как с помощью метрик на основе эталонов, так и с использованием самих LLM в качестве оценщиков. Полученные результаты показывают, что написание выводов существенно отличается от суммаризации, и выявляют важность выбора метода оценки для сравнения моделей.

en.title: Unlocking Biomedical Insights: MedConclusion Dataset for AI Reasoning
en.desc: This paper presents MedConclusion, a dataset containing 5.7 million structured abstracts from PubMed, aimed at enhancing biomedical conclusion generation. It allows researchers to evaluate large language models (LLMs) on their ability to derive conclusions from structured scientific evidence. The dataset pairs non-conclusion sections of abstracts with their corresponding author-written conclusions, providing a natural supervision mechanism for training models. The study also highlights the differences between conclusion writing and summary writing, revealing that current evaluation metrics may not effectively distinguish model performance.

zh.title: MedConclusion：生物医学结论生成的新数据集
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个大型数据集MedConclusion，包含570万条PubMed结构化摘要，用于生物医学结论生成。该数据集为评估大型语言模型在从结构化科学证据中推理的能力提供了基础。每个实例将摘要的非结论部分与原作者撰写的结论配对，提供了自然发生的监督信息。研究表明，结论写作与摘要写作在行为上有明显区别，且强模型在当前自动评估指标下表现相似。

papers.title: Training LLM Agents for Spontaneous, Reward-Free Self-Evolution via World Knowledge Exploration
papers.abstract: Abstract Agents equipped with intrinsic meta-evolution capabilities demonstrate improved performance on web navigation tasks through self-generated world knowledge without external supervision.  AI-generated summary Most agents today ``self-evolve'' by following rewards and rules defined by humans. However, this process remains fundamentally dependent on external supervision; without human guidance, the evolution stops. In this work, we train agents to possess an intrinsic meta-evolution capability to spontaneously learn about unseen environments prior to task execution.   To instill this ability, we design an outcome-based reward mechanism that measures how much an agent's self-generated world knowledge improves its success rate on downstream tasks. This reward signal is used exclusively during the training phase to teach the model how to explore and summarize effectively. At inference time, the agent requires no external rewards or human instructions. It spontaneously performs native self-evolution to adapt to unknown environments using its internal parameters.   When applied to Qwen3-30B and Seed-OSS-36B, this shift to native evolution yields a 20% performance increase on WebVoyager and WebWalker. Most strikingly, the generated world knowledge even enables a compact 14B Qwen3 model to outperform the unassisted Gemini-2.5-Flash, establishing a new paradigm for truly evolving agents.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)

ru.title: Агенты, которые учатся эволюционировать сами по себе
ru.desc: Исследование предлагает агентам способность к внутренней мета-эволюции, позволяя им самостоятельно генерировать знания об окружении без внешнего контроля во время вывода. Авторы разработали механизм вознаграждения на основе результатов, который оценивает, насколько самогенерированные знания об окружении улучшают успешность выполнения задач, обучая модель эффективно исследовать и анализировать информацию. Предложенный подход демонстрирует 20% улучшение производительности на задачах навигации по сайтам и позволяет более компактным моделям превзойти более крупные системы. Главное достижение заключается в том, что агенты достигают истинной эволюции, опираясь исключительно на внутренние параметры во время инференса.

en.title: Empowering Agents with Self-Driven Evolution for Enhanced Web Navigation
en.desc: This paper presents agents that can evolve their own knowledge without needing human guidance, enhancing their ability to navigate the web. These agents use a unique reward system that encourages them to learn about new environments before they start their tasks. By training with this intrinsic meta-evolution capability, the agents can adapt and improve their performance on various tasks independently. The results show significant performance gains, demonstrating a new approach to creating self-sufficient AI agents.

zh.title: 内在进化：智能体自我学习的新范式
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种具备内在元进化能力的智能体，能够在没有外部监督的情况下，通过自我生成的世界知识在网页导航任务中表现更好。传统的智能体依赖于人类定义的奖励和规则进行自我进化，但这种方法在没有人类指导时会停止进化。我们设计了一种基于结果的奖励机制，帮助智能体在任务执行前自发学习未知环境。实验结果表明，这种内在进化能力使得智能体在WebVoyager和WebWalker任务中性能提升了20%。

papers.title: MathNet: a Global Multimodal Benchmark for Mathematical Reasoning and Retrieval
papers.abstract: Abstract MathNet is a large-scale, multilingual, multimodal dataset of Olympiad-level math problems designed for evaluating mathematical reasoning and retrieval in generative models and embedding-based systems.  AI-generated summary Mathematical problem solving remains a challenging test of reasoning for large language and multimodal models, yet existing benchmarks are limited in size, language coverage, and task diversity. We introduce MathNet, a high-quality, large-scale, multimodal, and multilingual dataset of Olympiad-level math problems together with a benchmark for evaluating mathematical reasoning in generative models and mathematical retrieval in embedding-based systems. MathNet spans 47 countries, 17 languages, and two decades of competitions, comprising 30,676 expert-authored problems with solutions across diverse domains. In addition to the core dataset, we construct a retrieval benchmark consisting of mathematically equivalent and structurally similar problem pairs curated by human experts.   MathNet supports three tasks: (i) Problem Solving, (ii) Math-Aware Retrieval, and (iii) Retrieval-Augmented Problem Solving. Experimental results show that even state-of-the-art reasoning models (78.4% for Gemini-3.1-Pro and 69.3% for GPT-5) remain challenged, while embedding models struggle to retrieve equivalent problems. We further show that retrieval-augmented generation performance is highly sensitive to retrieval quality; for example, DeepSeek-V3.2-Speciale achieves gains of up to 12%, obtaining the highest scores on the benchmark. MathNet provides the largest high-quality Olympiad dataset together with the first benchmark for evaluating mathematical problem retrieval, and we publicly release both the dataset and benchmark at https://mathnet.mit.edu.

ru.title: Олимпиадная математика как мера настоящего интеллекта моделей
ru.desc: MathNet — это большой многоязычный и мультимодальный набор данных, содержащий 30 676 олимпиадных задач по математике из 47 стран на 17 языках, разработанный для оценки способности моделей к математическому рассуждению. Датасет включает три основные задачи: решение задач, поиск математически эквивалентных проблем и поиск с дополнением через генерацию. Результаты показывают, что даже самые продвинутые LLM-модели (Gemini и GPT-5) решают только 70-78% задач, а модели встраивания (embedding models) плохо справляются с поиском эквивалентных задач. Использование поиска для дополнения решения (retrieval-augmented generation) повышает качество ответов моделей на 12%, демонстрируя важность комбинирования поиска и генерации для сложного математического рассуждения.

en.title: MathNet: Elevating AI's Math Problem Solving Skills
en.desc: MathNet is a comprehensive dataset designed to enhance the evaluation of mathematical reasoning in AI models. It includes 30,676 expert-authored Olympiad-level math problems across 47 countries and 17 languages, making it a diverse resource for testing generative and embedding-based systems. The dataset supports three main tasks: Problem Solving, Math-Aware Retrieval, and Retrieval-Augmented Problem Solving, highlighting its versatility. Experimental results indicate that even advanced models face challenges, particularly in retrieval tasks, emphasizing the need for high-quality data in AI training.

zh.title: MathNet：数学推理的新基准
zh.desc: MathNet是一个大规模的多语言多模态数据集，专注于奥林匹克级别的数学问题，旨在评估生成模型和嵌入系统中的数学推理和检索能力。该数据集涵盖47个国家、17种语言，包含30,676个专家编写的问题及其解决方案，支持三种任务：问题解决、数学感知检索和增强检索问题解决。实验结果表明，即使是最先进的推理模型在处理这些问题时仍然面临挑战，而嵌入模型在检索等效问题时表现不佳。MathNet不仅提供了高质量的数学问题数据集，还首次建立了数学问题检索的基准，促进了相关研究的发展。

papers.title: Modeling Multiple Support Strategies within a Single Turn for Emotional Support Conversations
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-strategy utterance generation methods for emotional support conversations outperform single-strategy approaches by enabling multiple support strategies within individual utterances.  AI-generated summary Emotional Support Conversation (ESC) aims to assist individuals experiencing distress by generating empathetic and supportive dialogue. While prior work typically assumes that each supporter turn corresponds to a single strategy, real-world supportive communication often involves multiple strategies within a single utterance. In this paper, we revisit the ESC task by formulating it as multi-strategy utterance generation, where each utterance may contain one or more strategy-response pairs. We propose two generation methods: All-in-One, which predicts all strategy-response pairs in a single decoding step, and One-by-One, which iteratively generates strategy-response pairs until completion. Both methods are further enhanced with cognitive reasoning guided by reinforcement learning to improve strategy selection and response composition. We evaluate our models on the ESConv dataset under both utterance-level and dialogue-level settings. Experimental results show that our methods effectively model multi-strategy utterances and lead to improved supportive quality and dialogue success. To our knowledge, this work provides the first systematic empirical evidence that allowing multiple support strategies within a single utterance is both feasible and beneficial for emotional support conversations. All code and data will be publicly available at https://github.com/aliyun/qwen-dianjin.
affiliations: Qwen DianJin Team, Alibaba Cloud Computing
School of Computer Science and Technology, Soochow University

ru.title: Множественные стратегии в одном ответе: ключ к лучшей эмоциональной поддержке
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается новый подход к задаче диалога эмоциональной поддержки, где каждое высказывание может содержать несколько стратегий поддержки вместо одной. Авторы представляют две модели генерации: All-in-One, которая предсказывает все пары стратегия-ответ за один проход декодирования, и One-by-One, которая итеративно генерирует эти пары до завершения. Обе модели дополнительно улучшены с помощью когнитивного рассуждения, направляемого обучением с подкреплением для оптимизации выбора стратегий. Экспериментальные результаты на датасете ESConv показывают, что предложенные методы более эффективны и приводят к повышению качества поддержки и успешности диалога.

en.title: Empowering Emotional Support with Multi-Strategy Conversations
en.desc: This paper introduces a new approach to generating emotional support conversations by using multiple strategies within a single utterance. Traditional methods often limit responses to one strategy, but this research shows that combining strategies can enhance the quality of support provided. The authors propose two methods for generating these multi-strategy utterances: All-in-One, which creates all responses at once, and One-by-One, which builds responses step by step. By incorporating reinforcement learning for better strategy selection, the study demonstrates that these methods significantly improve the effectiveness of supportive dialogue.

zh.title: 多策略发言生成提升情感支持对话质量
zh.desc: 本文探讨了情感支持对话中的多策略发言生成方法，认为这种方法比单一策略更有效。研究表明，现实中的支持性交流往往在一个发言中包含多个策略，而不是仅依赖单一策略。我们提出了两种生成方法：All-in-One和One-by-One，前者一次性预测所有策略-响应对，后者则逐步生成。通过强化学习增强认知推理，这些方法在情感支持对话中显著提高了支持质量和对话成功率。

papers.title: The Geometric Canary: Predicting Steerability and Detecting Drift via Representational Stability
papers.abstract: Abstract Geometric stability measures predict language model controllability and detect structural degradation, with supervised variants excelling at steering prediction and unsupervised variants at drift detection.  AI-generated summary Reliable deployment of language models requires two capabilities that appear distinct but share a common geometric foundation: predicting whether a model will accept targeted behavioral control, and detecting when its internal structure degrades. We show that geometric stability, the consistency of a representation's pairwise distance structure, addresses both. Supervised Shesha variants that measure task-aligned geometric stability predict linear steerability with near-perfect accuracy (ρ= 0.89-0.97) across 35-69 embedding models and three NLP tasks, capturing unique variance beyond class separability (partial ρ= 0.62-0.76). A critical dissociation emerges: unsupervised stability fails entirely for steering on real-world tasks (ρapprox 0.10), revealing that task alignment is essential for controllability prediction. However, unsupervised stability excels at drift detection, measuring nearly 2times greater geometric change than CKA during post-training alignment (up to 5.23times in Llama) while providing earlier warning in 73\% of models and maintaining a 6times lower false alarm rate than Procrustes. Together, supervised and unsupervised stability form complementary diagnostics for the LLM deployment lifecycle: one for pre-deployment controllability assessment, the other for post-deployment monitoring.

ru.title: Геометрическая стабильность как универсальный диагностик для контроля и мониторинга языковых моделей
ru.desc: Работа предлагает метрику геометрической стабильности для оценки внутренних представлений языковых моделей. Авторы показывают, что контролируемые варианты этой метрики точно предсказывают возможность управления поведением модели (коэффициент корреляции 0.89-0.97), в то время как неконтролируемые варианты эффективно обнаруживают деградацию структуры модели при переобучении. Ключевое открытие заключается в том, что для предсказания управляемости необходимо выравнивание метрики с целевой задачей, тогда как обнаружение дрейфа работает лучше без такого выравнивания. Предложенный подход обеспечивает дополнительные инструменты для контроля жизненного цикла развёртывания больших языковых моделей: оценка управляемости перед развёртыванием и мониторинг стабильности после.

en.title: Harnessing Geometric Stability for Language Model Control and Monitoring
en.desc: This paper discusses geometric stability measures that help in understanding and controlling language models. It highlights two main tasks: predicting if a model can be controlled effectively and detecting when its internal structure is degrading. The authors demonstrate that supervised methods can accurately predict controllability, while unsupervised methods are better at detecting structural changes. Together, these methods provide a comprehensive approach for managing language models throughout their lifecycle.

zh.title: 几何稳定性：语言模型可控性与监测的关键
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了几何稳定性如何影响语言模型的可控性和结构退化的检测。研究表明，监督学习的几何稳定性测量可以准确预测模型的可控性，而无监督学习则在检测模型漂移方面表现优异。通过对不同嵌入模型和自然语言处理任务的分析，监督方法在可控性预测上达到了近乎完美的准确率。总的来说，监督和无监督的稳定性测量为语言模型的部署生命周期提供了互补的诊断工具。

papers.title: EvoMaster: A Foundational Agent Framework for Building Evolving Autonomous Scientific Agents at Scale
papers.abstract: Abstract EvoMaster is a scalable, self-evolving agent framework designed for large-scale scientific discovery that enables iterative hypothesis refinement and knowledge accumulation across experimental cycles.  AI-generated summary The convergence of large language models and agents is catalyzing a new era of scientific discovery: Agentic Science. While the scientific method is inherently iterative, existing agent frameworks are predominantly static, narrowly scoped, and lack the capacity to learn from trial and error. To bridge this gap, we present EvoMaster, a foundational evolving agent framework engineered specifically for Agentic Science at Scale. Driven by the core principle of continuous self-evolution, EvoMaster empowers agents to iteratively refine hypotheses, self-critique, and progressively accumulate knowledge across experimental cycles, faithfully mirroring human scientific inquiry. Crucially, as a domain-agnostic base harness, EvoMaster is exceptionally easy to scale up -- enabling developers to build and deploy highly capable, self-evolving scientific agents for arbitrary disciplines in approximately 100 lines of code. Built upon EvoMaster, we incubated the SciMaster ecosystem across domains such as machine learning, physics, and general science.
