Evaluations on four authoritative benchmarks (Humanity's Last Exam, MLE-Bench Lite, BrowseComp, and FrontierScience) demonstrate that EvoMaster achieves state-of-the-art scores of 41.1%, 75.8%, 73.3%, and 53.3%, respectively. It comprehensively outperforms the general-purpose baseline OpenClaw with relative improvements ranging from +159% to +316%, robustly validating its efficacy and generality as the premier foundational framework for the next generation of autonomous scientific discovery. EvoMaster is available at https://github.com/sjtu-sai-agents/EvoMaster.
affiliations: School of Artificial Intelligence, Shanghai Jiao Tong University

ru.title: Самоэволюционирующие агенты для научных открытий нового поколения
ru.desc: EvoMaster — это масштабируемая архитектура на основе LLM, предназначенная для автоматизации научных открытий через самоэволюцию агентов. Система позволяет агентам итеративно уточнять гипотезы, критиковать собственные результаты и накапливать знания на протяжении экспериментальных циклов, моделируя естественный научный метод. Фреймворк является доменно-независимым и требует минимального кода (около 100 строк) для развертывания специализированных научных агентов в различных областях. EvoMaster демонстрирует значительные улучшения на бенчмарках (от +159% до +316% относительно базовых моделей), подтверждая эффективность подхода для автономного научного исследования.

en.title: Empowering Scientific Discovery with Self-Evolving Agents
en.desc: EvoMaster is a cutting-edge framework designed to enhance scientific discovery through self-evolving agents. It allows these agents to refine hypotheses and accumulate knowledge over multiple experimental cycles, mimicking the iterative nature of human scientific inquiry. Unlike traditional static frameworks, EvoMaster is domain-agnostic and can be easily scaled, enabling rapid development of sophisticated agents across various scientific fields. Evaluations show that EvoMaster significantly outperforms existing models, establishing it as a leading tool for autonomous scientific exploration.

zh.title: EvoMaster：科学发现的新纪元
zh.desc: EvoMaster是一个可扩展的自我进化代理框架，旨在支持大规模科学发现。它允许在实验周期中进行迭代假设的细化和知识的积累，模拟人类的科学探究过程。与现有的静态代理框架不同，EvoMaster能够通过自我批评和学习来不断改进。该框架易于扩展，开发者只需约100行代码即可构建和部署强大的自我进化科学代理。

papers.title: Beyond Text-Dominance: Understanding Modality Preference of Omni-modal Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Research reveals that native omni-modal large language models exhibit visual preference over text, with modality preference emerging progressively in mid-to-late layers and enabling diagnosis of cross-modal hallucinations.  AI-generated summary Native Omni-modal Large Language Models (OLLMs) have shifted from pipeline architectures to unified representation spaces. However, this native integration gives rise to a critical yet underexplored phenomenon: modality preference. To bridge this gap, we first systematically quantify modality preference of OLLMs using a newly-curated conflict-based benchmark and the modality selection rate metric. Our evaluation of ten representative OLLMs reveals a notable paradigm shift: unlike the ``text-dominance'' of traditional VLMs, most OLLMs exhibit a pronounced visual preference. To further understand the underlying mechanism, we conduct layer-wise probing and demonstrate that such modality preference is not static but emerges progressively in the mid-to-late layers. Building upon these insights, we leverage these internal signals to diagnose cross-modal hallucinations, achieving competitive performance across three downstream multi-modal benchmarks without task-specific data. Our work provides both a mechanistic understanding and a practical tool for building more trustworthy OLLMs. Our code and related resources are publicly available at: https://github.com/icip-cas/OmniPreference
affiliations: Chinese Information Processing Laboratory, Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China

ru.title: Зрение побеждает текст: как мультимодальные модели выбирают между информацией
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что единые мультимодальные языковые модели проявляют предпочтение визуальной информации над текстом. Авторы разработали метрику выбора модальности и бенчмарк на основе конфликтующих данных для количественной оценки этого явления. Они обнаружили, что модальное предпочтение не является статичным, а возникает прогрессивно в средних и поздних слоях нейронной сети. Используя эти внутренние сигналы модели, исследователи создали метод диагностики кроссмодальных галлюцинаций, который показывает конкурентные результаты на нескольких бенчмарках без использования задачно-специфичных данных.

en.title: Visual Preference: A New Era for Omni-Modal Language Models
en.desc: This paper investigates the behavior of native omni-modal large language models (OLLMs) in terms of their preference for visual information over text. It highlights a significant shift from traditional models, which typically favor text, to OLLMs that show a strong visual preference, especially in their mid-to-late layers. The authors introduce a new benchmark to quantify this modality preference and demonstrate how it can be used to diagnose cross-modal hallucinations effectively. Their findings not only enhance the understanding of OLLMs but also provide tools for improving their reliability in multi-modal tasks.

zh.title: 全模态语言模型的视觉偏好探索
zh.desc: 本研究揭示了本地全模态大型语言模型（OLLMs）在视觉和文本之间存在偏好，且这种模态偏好在模型的中后层逐渐显现。我们通过新创建的基于冲突的基准和模态选择率指标，系统量化了OLLMs的模态偏好。与传统的视觉语言模型（VLMs）以文本为主的特征不同，大多数OLLMs表现出明显的视觉偏好。我们的研究不仅提供了对这一现象的机制理解，还为构建更可信的OLLMs提供了实用工具。

papers.title: MNAFT: modality neuron-aware fine-tuning of multimodal large language models for image translation
papers.abstract: Abstract Modality neuron-aware fine-tuning (MNAFT) enhances image translation by selectively updating specific neurons in multimodal large language models, preserving pre-trained knowledge while improving cross-modal understanding.  AI-generated summary Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have shown impressive capabilities, yet they often struggle to effectively capture the fine-grained textual information within images crucial for accurate image translation. This often leads to a modality gap between visual text inputs and textual inputs/outputs for image translation. Existing methods, primarily relying on instruction fine-tuning, risk parameter redundancy of pre-trained knowledge, hindering generalization performance. To address this, we introduce modality neuron-aware fine-tuning (MNAFT), a novel approach that takes advantage of the specialized roles of individual neurons within MLLMs for enhanced image translation. MNAFT identifies language-agnostic and language-specific neurons in both vision and language modules through an instruction-driven activation analysis, evaluating their importance in various translation tasks. We then perform selective fine-tuning, updating only the parameters of language-specific and language-agnostic neurons within the selected layers relevant to the target task, while preserving the knowledge encoded in other neurons and layers. Our extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that MNAFT significantly outperforms state-of-the-art image translation methods, including cascaded models, standard full fine-tuning, and parameter-efficient tuning techniques. Furthermore, we provide comprehensive analysis, including visualizations of neuron activations and clustering patterns, to offer insights into the roles of different neuron groups in mediating cross-modal understanding and facilitating accurate language-specific translation.
affiliations: School of Artificial Intelligence, Shanghai Jiao Tong University
School of Computer Science and Technology, Tianjin University
School of Information Resource Management, Renmin University of China

ru.title: Осознанная нейронами тонкая настройка для точного мультимодального перевода
ru.desc: Работа представляет метод MNAFT для улучшения перевода изображений с помощью мультимодальных больших языковых моделей. Авторы выявляют специализированные нейроны в визуальных и языковых модулях модели, разделяя их на языково-зависимые и языково-независимые с использованием анализа активации. Вместо полного дообучения модели, метод выборочно обновляет только параметры значимых нейронов, сохраняя знания в остальной части сети. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что MNAFT значительно превосходит существующие методы перевода изображений, обеспечивая лучшее понимание взаимодействия между модальностями и повышая качество перевода текста из изображений.

en.title: Enhancing Image Translation with Selective Neuron Fine-Tuning
en.desc: This paper presents Modality Neuron-Aware Fine-Tuning (MNAFT), a technique that improves image translation by selectively updating specific neurons in multimodal large language models (MLLMs). MNAFT addresses the challenge of modality gaps between visual and textual inputs by identifying and fine-tuning language-specific and language-agnostic neurons. This selective approach preserves the pre-trained knowledge of the model while enhancing its ability to understand and translate images accurately. Experimental results show that MNAFT outperforms existing image translation methods, demonstrating its effectiveness in bridging the gap between different modalities.

zh.title: 模态神经元感知微调：提升图像翻译的新方法
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的方法，称为模态神经元感知微调（MNAFT），旨在改善图像翻译的效果。MNAFT通过选择性地更新多模态大语言模型中的特定神经元，来增强跨模态理解，同时保留预训练知识。该方法通过分析神经元的激活情况，识别出与语言无关和与语言相关的神经元，从而进行有针对性的微调。实验结果表明，MNAFT在多个基准测试中显著优于现有的图像翻译方法。

papers.title: Geometric coherence of single-cell CRISPR perturbations reveals regulatory architecture and predicts cellular stress
papers.abstract: Abstract  Genome engineering has achieved remarkable sequence-level precision, yet predicting the transcriptomic state that a cell will occupy after perturbation remains an open problem. Single-cell CRISPR screens measure how far cells move from their unperturbed state, but this effect magnitude ignores a fundamental question: do the cells move together? Two perturbations with identical magnitude can produce qualitatively different outcomes if one drives cells coherently along a shared trajectory while the other scatters them across expression space. We introduce a geometric stability metric, Shesha, that quantifies the directional coherence of single-cell perturbation responses as the mean cosine similarity between individual cell shift vectors and the mean perturbation direction. Across five CRISPR datasets (2,200+ perturbations spanning CRISPRa, CRISPRi, and pooled screens), stability correlates strongly with effect magnitude (Spearman ρ=0.75-0.97), with a calibrated cross-dataset correlation of 0.97. Crucially, discordant cases where the two metrics decouple expose regulatory architecture: pleiotropic master regulators such as CEBPA and GATA1 pay a "geometric tax," producing large but incoherent shifts, while lineage-specific factors such as KLF1 produce tightly coordinated responses. After controlling for magnitude, geometric instability is independently associated with elevated chaperone activation (HSPA5/BiP; ρ_{partial}=-0.34 and -0.21 across datasets), and the high-stability/high-stress quadrant is systematically depleted. The magnitude-stability relationship persists in scGPT foundation model embeddings, confirming it is a property of biological state space rather than linear projection. Perturbation stability provides a complementary axis for hit prioritization in screens, phenotypic quality control in cell manufacturing, and evaluation of in silico perturbation predictions.

ru.title: Геометрическая стабильность возмущений раскрывает архитектуру регуляции генов
ru.desc: Статья представляет новую метрику Shesha для оценки согласованности ответов клеток на генетические возмущения в экспериментах CRISPR. Метрика измеряет направленную когерентность как среднее косинусное сходство между векторами сдвигов отдельных клеток и средним направлением возмущения. Анализ показал, что плеиотропные регуляторы (такие как CEBPA и GATA1) вызывают большие, но некогерентные сдвиги, в то время как специфичные для линии дифференцировки факторы (например KLF1) производят согласованные ответы. Метрика стабильности возмущения предоставляет дополнительную ось для приоритизации результатов скрининга и контроля качества при производстве клеток.

en.title: Measuring Coherence in CRISPR Perturbation Responses
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenge of predicting how cells will respond at the transcriptomic level after genetic modifications using CRISPR technology. It introduces a new metric called Shesha, which measures the coherence of cell responses to perturbations by analyzing the similarity of their movement in expression space. The study finds a strong correlation between the stability of these responses and the magnitude of the effects, revealing insights into the regulatory mechanisms at play. By understanding the geometric stability of perturbations, researchers can improve hit prioritization in genetic screens and enhance the quality of cell manufacturing processes.

zh.title: 扰动稳定性：细胞反应的新视角
zh.desc: 基因组工程在序列级别上取得了显著的精确度，但预测细胞在扰动后所处的转录组状态仍然是一个未解决的问题。我们提出了一种几何稳定性度量Shesha，用于量化单细胞扰动反应的方向一致性。通过分析五个CRISPR数据集，我们发现稳定性与效应大小之间存在强相关性，并且在不同数据集之间的相关性也很高。该研究揭示了调控架构的复杂性，并为细胞制造和计算扰动预测的评估提供了新的视角。

papers.title: Latent Preference Modeling for Cross-Session Personalized Tool Calling
papers.abstract: Abstract Personalized tool calling in LLM-based agents is improved through memory-augmented methods that capture user choice reasoning rather than just choices, using minimal token overhead.  AI-generated summary Users often omit essential details in their requests to LLM-based agents, resulting in under-specified inputs for tool use. This poses a fundamental challenge for tool-augmented agents, as API execution typically requires complete arguments, highlighting the need for personalized tool calling. To study this problem, we introduce MPT, a benchmark comprising 265 multi-session dialogues that cover three challenges: Preference Recall, Preference Induction, and Preference Transfer. We also propose PRefine, a test-time memory-augmented method that represents user preferences as evolving hypotheses. Through a generate--verify--refine loop, it extracts reusable constraints from history and improves tool-calling accuracy while using only 1.24% of the tokens required by full-history prompting. These results indicate that robust personalization in agentic systems depends on memory that captures the reasons behind user choices, not just the choices themselves.
affiliations: Hanyang University, Seoul, Republic of Korea

ru.title: Персонализация через память о причинах, а не о выборах
ru.desc: В статье решается проблема персонализации инструментов в LLM-агентах, когда пользователи предоставляют неполные запросы. Авторы представляют MPT - бенчмарк из 265 многосеансовых диалогов, охватывающих три задачи: припоминание предпочтений, индукцию предпочтений и трансфер предпочтений. Предложен метод PRefine с дополненной памятью, который представляет предпочтения пользователя как эволюционирующие гипотезы и извлекает переиспользуемые ограничения из истории через цикл генерация-проверка-уточнение. Метод улучшает точность вызова инструментов, используя всего 1.24% токенов от полного промпта истории.

en.title: Enhancing Personalization in LLM Agents with Memory-Driven Insights
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenge of personalized tool calling in large language model (LLM)-based agents by introducing memory-augmented methods. It highlights that users often provide incomplete information, leading to difficulties in executing API calls that require full arguments. The authors present MPT, a benchmark with 265 dialogues to evaluate three key challenges related to user preferences. They propose PRefine, a method that captures user preferences as evolving hypotheses, enhancing tool-calling accuracy while minimizing token usage.

zh.title: 个性化工具调用的记忆增强方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种改进的个性化工具调用方法，利用增强记忆技术捕捉用户选择背后的推理，而不仅仅是选择本身。研究表明，用户在向基于大语言模型的代理请求时，常常遗漏关键信息，导致输入不完整，从而影响工具的使用效果。为了解决这一问题，本文引入了MPT基准，包含265个多会话对话，涵盖了偏好回忆、偏好归纳和偏好转移等挑战。我们还提出了PRefine方法，通过生成-验证-精炼的循环，提取历史中的可重用约束，从而提高工具调用的准确性，且仅使用了完整历史提示所需的1.24%的令牌。

papers.title: Multiplication in Multimodal LLMs: Computation with Text, Image, and Audio Inputs
papers.abstract: Abstract Multimodal large language models demonstrate consistent computational limitations in exact multi-digit multiplication across different representations and modalities, with performance closely tied to a novel arithmetic load metric that predicts accuracy better than traditional step-counting methods.  AI-generated summary Multimodal LLMs can accurately perceive numerical content across modalities yet fail to perform exact multi-digit multiplication when the identical underlying arithmetic problem is presented as numerals, number words, images, or in audio form. Because existing benchmarks often lack systematically paired instances across modalities, it remains difficult to compare genuine arithmetic limits within and across model families. We therefore introduce a controlled multimodal multiplication benchmark that factorially varies digit length, digit sparsity, representation (e.g., numerals vs. number words), and modality (text, rendered images, audio), with paired instances from a reproducible generator. We also define arithmetic load, C, as the product of the total and non-zero digit count as a compact, mechanistically motivated proxy for operation count. Across evaluations, accuracy falls sharply as C grows, often nearing zero by C > 100. Indeed, C remains predictive of performance across modalities and models, with R-squared often > 0.5, nearing the value from more complex measures of arithmetic load that count the number of intermediate arithmetic steps. A separate perception-versus-computation decomposition shows that multimodal degradation is primarily computational rather than perceptual: on matched-perception checks, models are near-perfect (> 99%) across modalities, even when multiplication accuracy drops. Beyond measuring when models fail, we ask which procedures they are predisposed to follow. We introduce a forced-completion loss probe that scores heuristic-specific reasoning prefixes--including columnar multiplication, distributive decomposition, and rounding/compensation. Here, decomposition is favored in both text and vision modalities; heuristic-specific LoRA adapters produce near-orthogonal updates yet degrade accuracy, indicating the base model maintains a well-tuned internal router.

ru.title: Граница арифметического мышления мультимодальных нейросетей
ru.desc: В работе исследуются вычислительные ограничения мультимодальных больших языковых моделей при выполнении точного умножения многозначных чисел. Авторы создали контролируемый бенчмарк, варьирующий количество цифр, их разреженность и модальность представления (текст, изображения, аудио), чтобы систематически оценить арифметические способности. Предложена новая метрика «арифметическая нагрузка», определённая как произведение общего числа цифр и количества ненулевых цифр, которая лучше предсказывает точность моделей, чем традиционные методы подсчёта шагов. Анализ показал, что снижение производительности в основном обусловлено вычислительными, а не перцептивными ограничениями моделей.

en.title: Unveiling the Arithmetic Limits of Multimodal LLMs
en.desc: This paper investigates the limitations of multimodal large language models (LLMs) in performing exact multi-digit multiplication across various formats like text, images, and audio. The authors introduce a new benchmark that systematically tests these models on multiplication tasks while varying factors such as digit length and representation. They define a metric called 'arithmetic load' to predict model performance, finding that accuracy declines significantly as this load increases. The study reveals that the degradation in performance is primarily due to computational challenges rather than perceptual issues, suggesting that while models can perceive numbers well, they struggle with the arithmetic operations required for accurate multiplication.

zh.title: 多模态模型的算术计算挑战
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了多模态大型语言模型在多位数乘法计算中的局限性。尽管这些模型能够识别数字内容，但在面对相同的算术问题时，它们在不同表现形式（如数字、文字、图像或音频）下的准确性却显著下降。研究者们引入了一种新的多模态乘法基准，系统地变化数字长度、稀疏性和表现形式，以便更好地比较模型的算术能力。通过定义算术负载作为操作计数的代理，研究发现随着算术负载的增加，模型的准确性急剧下降，表明计算能力是主要的限制因素。

papers.title: Back to Repair: A Minimal Denoising Network\ for Time Series Anomaly Detection
papers.abstract: Abstract JuRe, a simple denoising network for time series anomaly detection, demonstrates that architectural simplicity can match or exceed complex models when the training objective properly implements the manifold-projection principle.  AI-generated summary We introduce JuRe (Just Repair), a minimal denoising network for time series anomaly detection that exposes a central finding: architectural complexity is unnecessary when the training objective correctly implements the manifold-projection principle. JuRe consists of a single depthwise-separable convolutional residual block with hidden dimension 128, trained to repair corrupted time series windows and scored at inference by a fixed, parameter-free structural discrepancy function. Despite using no attention, no latent variable, and no adversarial component, JuRe ranks second on the TSB-AD multivariate benchmark (AUC-PR 0.404, 180 series, 17 datasets) and second on the UCR univariate archive by AUC-PR (0.198, 250 series), leading all neural baselines on AUC-PR and VUS-PR. Component ablation on TSB-AD identifies training-time corruption as the dominant factor (ΔAUC-PR = 0.047 on removal), confirming that the denoising objective, not network capacity, drives detection quality. Pairwise Wilcoxon signed-rank tests establish statistical significance against 21 of 25 baselines on TSB-AD. Code is available at the URL https://github.com/iis-esslingen/JuRe.

ru.title: Простота побеждает сложность: принцип проекции на многообразие для обнаружения аномалий
ru.desc: JuRe — это простая сеть автоматического кодирования для обнаружения аномалий в временных рядах, которая использует принцип проекции на многообразие через задачу восстановления поврежденных последовательностей. Модель состоит из одного блока с разделяемыми по глубине сверточными слоями и скрытым размером 128, обучаясь исправлять искаженные окна временных рядов без внимания, латентных переменных и состязательных компонентов. При инференсе система использует фиксированную параметризованную функцию структурного несоответствия для выявления аномалий. Несмотря на архитектурную простоту, JuRe демонстрирует конкурентные результаты на бенчмарках TSB-AD и UCR, превосходя все нейросетевые базовые модели по метрикам AUC-PR и VUS-PR.

en.title: Simplicity in Architecture, Power in Detection
en.desc: JuRe is a straightforward denoising network designed for detecting anomalies in time series data. It shows that simpler architectures can perform just as well as more complex models if the training goal aligns with the manifold-projection principle. The network uses a single depthwise-separable convolutional residual block and focuses on repairing corrupted time series segments. Its performance on benchmark datasets demonstrates that the denoising objective is crucial for effective anomaly detection, rather than the complexity of the model itself.

zh.title: 简单架构，卓越检测：JuRe的力量
zh.desc: JuRe是一种简单的去噪网络，用于时间序列异常检测。它表明，当训练目标正确实现流形投影原则时，架构的复杂性并不是必要的。JuRe由一个深度可分离卷积残差块组成，隐藏维度为128，旨在修复损坏的时间序列窗口。尽管没有使用注意力机制、潜变量或对抗组件，JuRe在多个基准测试中表现优异，证明了去噪目标对检测质量的重要性。

papers.title: HSG: Hyperbolic Scene Graph
papers.abstract: Abstract Hyperbolic Scene Graph (HSG) improves scene graph modeling by learning embeddings in hyperbolic space, enhancing hierarchical structure quality and retrieval performance through natural encoding of hierarchical relationships.  AI-generated summary Scene graph representations enable structured visual understanding by modeling objects and their relationships, and have been widely used for multiview and 3D scene reasoning. Existing methods such as MSG learn scene graph embeddings in Euclidean space using contrastive learning and attention based association. However, Euclidean geometry does not explicitly capture hierarchical entailment relationships between places and objects, limiting the structural consistency of learned representations. To address this, we propose Hyperbolic Scene Graph (HSG), which learns scene graph embeddings in hyperbolic space where hierarchical relationships are naturally encoded through geometric distance. Our results show that HSG improves hierarchical structure quality while maintaining strong retrieval performance. The largest gains are observed in graph level metrics: HSG achieves a PP IoU of 33.17 and the highest Graph IoU of 33.51, outperforming the best AoMSG variant (25.37) by 8.14, highlighting the effectiveness of hyperbolic representation learning for scene graph modeling. Code: https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/HSG.
affiliations: School of Computer Science, Peking University

ru.title: Гиперболическое пространство для иерархически структурированных графов сцен
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Hyperbolic Scene Graph (HSG) для улучшения моделирования графов сцен путем обучения представлений в гиперболическом пространстве вместо традиционного евклидова. Гиперболическая геометрия естественным образом кодирует иерархические отношения между объектами и местами, что лучше отражает структуру реальных сцен. Авторы используют контрастное обучение в гиперболическом пространстве для получения более структурированных и иерархически согласованных эмбеддингов графов сцен. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение в метриках качества иерархической структуры (Graph IoU: 33.51 против 25.37 у базовых методов) при сохранении высокой производительности поиска.

en.title: Unlocking Hierarchical Relationships with Hyperbolic Scene Graphs
en.desc: The Hyperbolic Scene Graph (HSG) paper introduces a new way to model scene graphs by using hyperbolic space instead of traditional Euclidean space. This approach allows for better representation of hierarchical relationships between objects and places, which is crucial for understanding complex scenes. By learning embeddings in hyperbolic space, HSG enhances the quality of hierarchical structures and improves retrieval performance in scene graph tasks. The results demonstrate significant improvements in graph-level metrics, showcasing the advantages of hyperbolic representation for scene graph modeling.

zh.title: 超曲率场景图：提升层次结构与检索性能的创新方法
zh.desc: 超曲率场景图（HSG）通过在超曲率空间中学习嵌入，提升了场景图建模的效果。它通过自然编码层次关系，增强了层次结构的质量和检索性能。与现有的欧几里得空间方法相比，HSG能够更好地捕捉对象和场所之间的层次关系。实验结果表明，HSG在图级别指标上表现优异，显著提高了结构一致性。
